$schema_note: Static detection map for `detect_libraries.py`. Maps installed Python package names (covers_dependencies) to Monte Carlo OpenTelemetry instrumentor packages so the skill can recognize what to install for a given project offline. This file is NOT a fallback for `fetch_sdk_docs.py`: PyPI is the source of truth for the live supported-instrumentor list. The version_constraint here is a last-known-compatible value used to pin transitive resolution; re-run fetch_sdk_docs.py to confirm the current PyPI value. additional_pins are transitive constraints PyPI metadata does not expose (e.g. wrapt<2 for OpenLLMetry instrumentors that pass module= to wrap_function_wrapper, which wrapt 2.x renamed to target=).

supported_instrumentors.notes: LangGraph traces are produced by the same opentelemetry-instrumentation-langchain package.

boto3 covers many AWS services; detect_libraries.py routes boto3 matches to `unsupported` rather than `detected` since it cannot tell from deps alone whether the customer is calling Bedrock. The skill should ask the user before installing this instrumentor.
Verify on PyPI at runtime — package availability has historically been less stable than the LangChain/OpenAI/Anthropic family.
Verify on PyPI at runtime — package name and version constraint may have changed.
